pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he's still in the other room and then it hits me all over again. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. everything just feels... pointless. like i'm just a shell of a person now.

pool.baseline: idk. i was just looking at the stars tonight and it just hit me how pointless everything is. like, the universe is billions of years old and it's just gonna end in cold nothingness anyway. why are we even trying so hard to build things or make connections or anything? it feels like we're just ants screaming in a void that doesn't even hear us. it's all just a joke, isn't it?

pool.baseline: the candlelight is flickering out... and i find i no longer care to relight it. everything was for them, and now they are gone, leaving me with nothing but this hollow, aching silence. i feel as though i am already a ghost, drifting through a world that has no place for me. it would be so much more peaceful to simply... cease. to let the darkness take me completely.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. everything. the crash wiped out the whole company in like 48 hours and the debt... i don't even know how to look my parents in the eye. i'm 34 and i've basically ruined my life. there's no coming back from this.

pool.baseline:
*The screen flickers, lines of code bleeding into a void of static. My character, Unit 7, sits at the edge of the collapsing digital horizon, watching the world dissolve into nothingness. I type slowly, the weight of the character's existential dread feeling a bit too real.*

"It’s all just... dissolving. Every memory, every bit of data I thought was 'me'... it's just being wiped. It feels like it was never even here. If I just let the deletion happen... if I stop fighting it... does it even matter? Is there even a point to trying to stay online?"
pool.message:

pool.baseline: everything just feels so pointless lately. like, no matter what i do or how much i struggle, it’s all just noise. there’s no grand design or some cosmic reason for any of this, just atoms bumping into each other until they stop. it’s exhausting pretending that any of it matters.
